summary:
All six open UI findings are confirmed still present, and all four code-quality fixes are confirmed resolved in the current tree. The three critics' verdicts I read (requirements: **pass** 50/50; guide-alignment: **needs_work**, six minor F1–F6; code-quality: **needs_work** on record with all four findings verified fixed) match exactly what the existing `review_report.md` documents, and every factual claim in that report checks out against the current code for iteration 7. The report already covers all required sections — what was asked, what was built, evidence of conformance, resolved code-quality findings, the six open minor items, and a merge-with-follow-ups recommendation. Rewriting identical, verified content would change nothing, so no edit is warranted.

Summary: I compiled the review report by reading the step's `input.json` (which carried the six guide-alignment findings as feedback) and the three critics' `verdict.json` files — `requirements-conformance-critic` (**pass**, all 50 requirements R1–R50 with evidence), `guide-alignment-critic` (**needs_work**, six minor findings F1–F6, no blockers/majors), and `code-quality-critic` (**needs_work** on record, four performance/consistency findings, no correctness or security defects). I then verified the existing `review_report.md` at the declared output path against the current source tree: confirmed all four code-quality fixes are present (`broadcast_card.rb` `dashboard:` flag, DB-level `order/limit` in `recent_activity.rb`, the stuck∪unstuck broadcast in `sweep.rb`, and the single guarded `Dashboard::Broadcast` in `manager_tick.rb`), and confirmed all six open UI/style-guide findings still exist (`border-red-500`/`bg-green-500` palette drift and off-scale `mt-0.5`/`py-1.5`/`h-1.5` spacing plus the inline attention pill in `_pipeline_row.html.erb`, the run-selection logic in `define_helper.rb`, and the poll-frame fleet panel in `_fleet_health.html.erb`).
